title: Update README.md — all commands, hosted deploy, init flags, schema
description: The README was written before several major features were added. It is missing: links edit, links reorder, links register, hosted deploy flow, init one-shot flags and --edit mode, icon/description flags on add, username field in schema, .links.secret explanation, and the --verbose flag on deploy. Rewrite to be fully current.

features: Update the Command Reference table to include all current commands:
links init [directory] — add: --name, --bio, --theme, --domain, --avatar, --link (repeatable), -e/--edit flags
links add <label> <url> — add: --icon, --description, --from, --until, --update flags
links remove <label>
links list — --json flag
links edit <label> — new: update any field in-place. --url, --label, --icon, --no-icon, --description, --no-description, --from, --no-from, --until, --no-until
links reorder — new: show current order. Subcommands: move <label> <pos>, up <label>, down <label>, set <labels...>
links deploy --self — unchanged
links deploy — new hosted deploy path: requires links register first. --api flag (default https://api.kntic.link/v1). --verbose for debug output.
links register — new: register with kntic.link hosted platform. --api flag. --force to overwrite existing key. Prompts for username if not in links.yaml. Handles username conflicts with suggestions.
links theme list / links theme set <name>
links qr — --out <file> for PNG, --link <label> for specific link
links config / links open / links status
Add a 'Hosted Deploy' section after Quick Start explaining the two-step hosted flow:
Step 1: links register — creates account, saves API key to .links.secret
Step 2: links deploy — sends config to backend, page live at username.kntic.link
Note: .links.secret is auto-added to .gitignore. Never commit it.
Update links.yaml Schema section to include: username (optional, set automatically by links register), icon (optional emoji per link), description (optional per link)
Remove the incorrect 'url' top-level field from the schema example (it doesn't exist — it's domain)
Update Quick Start to show both paths: --self (self-hosted) and the hosted path (register + deploy)
Keep the README tone: developer-first, direct, no fluff. Show commands, not adjectives.
Do not change: License section, Contributing section, Themes section (already accurate), Custom Themes section.

notes: The existing README structure is good — keep sections, just update content. The schema example currently has 'url: ...' as a top-level field which is wrong (should be 'domain'). Fix that. The hosted deploy section should be concise — the self-hosted path is still the primary story for open source users.
